title: API Authentication Guide
author: Priya Nair
excerpt: How to authenticate requests against the OpsMind API using short-lived bearer tokens and refresh tokens.
content:
# API Authentication Guide

All requests to the OpsMind API must include a bearer token issued by the auth service.

## Requesting a token

```bash
curl -X POST https://api.opsmind.ai/v1/auth/token \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"client_id": "YOUR_CLIENT_ID", "client_secret": "YOUR_SECRET"}'
```

The response contains an access token and a refresh token:

```json
{
  "access_token": "eyJhbGciOi...",
  "refresh_token": "dGhpc2lz...",
  "expires_in": 3600
}
```

## Using the token

Include it on every request:

```bash
curl https://api.opsmind.ai/v1/documents \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer eyJhbGciOi..."
```

## Token lifetimes

| Token type | Lifetime | Rotation |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Access token | 1 hour | Automatic on refresh |
| Refresh token | 30 days | Rotates on each use |
| API key (service accounts) | No expiry | Manual |

## Best practices

- Never commit tokens to source control.
- Store refresh tokens in a secrets manager, not localStorage.
- Rotate service-account API keys at least every 90 days.
- Scope tokens to the minimum permissions needed.

See also the [rate limiting guide](https://example.com/docs/rate-limits) for request quotas.

title: New Hire Onboarding Checklist
author: Elena Torres
excerpt: Everything a new team member needs to set up in their first week, from accounts to team intros.
content:
# New Hire Onboarding Checklist

Welcome to the team! Work through this list during your first week.

## Week 1

- [x] Get your laptop and accounts provisioned
- [x] Join #general and #engineering on Slack
- [ ] Set up your development environment
- [ ] Complete the security awareness training
- [ ] Meet with your onboarding buddy

## Accounts you'll need

1. Google Workspace
2. GitHub (request access via IT)
3. Linear
4. VPN client

## Resources

- [Engineering setup guide](https://example.com/docs/dev-setup)
- [Team directory](https://example.com/team)

Questions? Ask in #onboarding at any time.

title: Incident Response Runbook
author: Sam Okafor
excerpt: Step-by-step process for triaging, communicating, and resolving production incidents.
content:
# Incident Response Runbook

## Severity levels

| Severity | Definition | Response time |
| --- | --- | --- |
| SEV1 | Full outage or data loss | Immediate |
| SEV2 | Major feature degraded | 30 minutes |
| SEV3 | Minor issue, workaround exists | Next business day |

## Steps

1. Acknowledge the page within 5 minutes.
2. Open an incident channel: `#incident-<date>`.
3. Post an initial status update within 10 minutes.
4. Identify and apply mitigation.
5. Confirm recovery with monitoring.
6. Write a postmortem within 48 hours.

## Useful commands

```bash
# Check service health
kubectl get pods -n production

# Tail recent logs
kubectl logs -n production -l app=api --tail=200 -f

# Roll back the last deploy
kubectl rollout undo deployment/api -n production
```

> Always page a second responder for SEV1 incidents — never triage alone.

title: Billing FAQ
author: Jordan Blake
excerpt: Answers to the most common billing questions our support team receives.
content:
# Billing FAQ

## When am I charged?

Subscriptions renew monthly on the date you first subscribed. You can see your next billing date under **Settings → Billing**.

## Can I get a refund?

We offer prorated refunds within 14 days of a charge for annual plans. Monthly plans are non-refundable but can be cancelled anytime to stop future charges.

## How do I update my payment method?

Go to **Settings → Billing → Payment method** and click *Update*. Changes apply to your next invoice.

## Do you offer nonprofit or education discounts?

Yes — 30% off for verified nonprofits and educational institutions. Email billing@opsmind.ai with proof of status.

## Where do I find past invoices?

All invoices are listed under **Settings → Billing → Invoice history**, and are also emailed automatically.

title: Database Schema Reference
author: Priya Nair
excerpt: Reference for the core production tables, their columns, and how they relate to each other.
content:
# Database Schema Reference

## `documents` table

| Column | Type | Notes |
| --- | --- | --- |
| id | uuid | Primary key |
| title | text | |
| folder_id | uuid | Foreign key → folders.id |
| author_id | uuid | Foreign key → users.id |
| updated_at | timestamptz | |

## `folders` table

| Column | Type | Notes |
| --- | --- | --- |
| id | uuid | Primary key |
| name | text | |
| parent_id | uuid, nullable | Self-referencing |

## Example query

```sql
SELECT d.title, f.name AS folder
FROM documents d
JOIN folders f ON f.id = d.folder_id
WHERE d.updated_at >= NOW() - INTERVAL '30 days'
ORDER BY d.updated_at DESC;
```

Schema migrations live in `db/migrations` and run automatically on deploy.

title: On-call Escalation Policy
author: Sam Okafor
excerpt: Who gets paged, in what order, and how long we wait before escalating further.
content:
# On-call Escalation Policy

## Escalation chain

1. Primary on-call engineer (page immediately)
2. Secondary on-call engineer (after 10 minutes, no ack)
3. Engineering manager (after 20 minutes, no ack)
4. VP Engineering (SEV1 only, after 30 minutes)

## Rotation

Rotations run weekly, Monday 9am to Monday 9am, and are managed in the on-call scheduling tool. Swaps must be requested at least 24 hours in advance.

## Compensation

On-call weeks include a stipend, and any incident work outside business hours is compensated as overtime per the standard policy.

title: Support Macro — Login Issues
author: Jordan Blake
excerpt: Canned response for the most common login troubleshooting steps.
content:
# Support Macro — Login Issues

Hi {{customer_name}},

Sorry you're having trouble logging in! A few things to try:

1. Reset your password from the [sign-in page](https://example.com/reset).
2. Clear your browser cache and cookies for our site.
3. Try an incognito/private window in case an extension is interfering.

If none of that works, reply here and I'll take a closer look at your account.

{{agent_name}}

title: Compliance Checklist — SOC 2
author: Priya Nair
excerpt: Controls and evidence we track continuously to stay SOC 2 Type II compliant.
content:
# Compliance Checklist — SOC 2

## Access control

- [x] MFA enforced for all employee accounts
- [x] Quarterly access reviews
- [ ] Automated de-provisioning on offboarding

## Change management

- [x] All production changes go through code review
- [x] Deploys are logged and attributable to a person

## Monitoring

- [x] Centralized logging with 1-year retention
- [x] Alerting on anomalous access patterns

Evidence for each control is collected automatically and stored in the compliance workspace ahead of the annual audit.

title: Sprint Retro Notes — Product
author: Marcus Webb
excerpt: What went well, what didn't, and action items from the latest sprint retrospective.
content:
# Sprint Retro Notes — Product

## Went well

- Shipped the new filter UI ahead of schedule
- Good cross-team sync with Engineering on the API changes

## Didn't go well

- Scope crept on the export feature mid-sprint
- QA was brought in too late

## Action items

1. Lock scope by day 2 of the sprint
2. Involve QA in planning, not just execution
